title: gastrochilus dalzellianus
intro: Gastrochilus dalzellianus, also known as Dalzell's orchid, is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Gastrochilus dalzellianus include the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
